---
title: Calendar View
description: See your entire release schedule at a glance -- spot conflicts, find gaps, and keep your marketing timeline tight.
---

The Calendar page gives you a bird's-eye view of every release date and marketing activity across your roster. Instead of flipping between spreadsheets and distributor dashboards, you see everything in one place.

![Calendar month view showing release dates and marketing activities](../../../assets/screenshots/calendar.png)

## Views

The calendar supports two views:

- **Month view** -- see the full month at a glance. This is where you spot problems like two releases dropping the same week or a month with nothing scheduled.
- **Week view** -- zoom into a specific week to check that your marketing activities are properly spaced. If you have a single dropping Friday, you can see whether pre-save posts, playlist pitches, and press outreach are all queued up.

## What appears on the calendar

- **Release dates** -- shown with the release cover art and title, so you can visually scan your schedule
- **Marketing activities** -- every promotional activity with a scheduled date, across all releases

Hover over any item to see a quick info card with artwork, title, and type.

## Filtering

Filter calendar events by type:

- Show only releases -- useful when you are planning your Q1 schedule and want to see how releases are distributed
- Show only marketing activities -- helpful for checking that your promo cadence is consistent
- Show both (default)

## Navigation

- Click on any release to jump directly to its detail page
- Use arrow buttons to move between months or weeks
- Click "Today" to jump back to the current date

## How to use it effectively

- **Avoid release collisions.** If you are running a label or managing multiple artists, the month view immediately shows when you have two releases competing for the same week. Spread them out so each project gets proper attention.
- **Spot gaps in your marketing timeline.** A release with no marketing activities in the two weeks before drop day is a missed opportunity. The calendar makes gaps obvious.
- **Coordinate across your roster.** Marketing activities from all releases appear together, so you can see your overall promotional workload and make sure your team is not overloaded in any given week.
